channel.title: Methodology on Xing Lin
channel.description: Recent content in Methodology on Xing Lin
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Trade-off Between Accuracy and Runtime Overhead
item.description: During the weekly technical forum within the NetApp ATG group, a team member presented the trip report for OSDI ‘14 this year. In his talk, he mentioned a paper that pushed me to think more in this line. The paper is about how to estimate the working set of a workload, using less memory and runs much faster. The only related work that dealt with the same problem was proposed more than 10 years ago and in that work, the authors tried to get the exact working set size, with a very high demand for memory and runtime. So, in their work, they tried to estimate it, with a goal to reduce memory demand and runtime. They got very impressive results. Together with other papers, I suddenly realized that it is actually quite common in compute science community, to take this approach: when it is too expensive to get the accurate result, it is usually a good time to think about how to approximate it with much lower overhead.
